contains_reference_count: How many category intervals include reference value {reference_value}?
Count the intervals whose lower-to-upper span contains reference value {reference_value}.
Using the printed interval endpoints, how many categories include {reference_value}?
For reference value {reference_value}, how many interval whiskers contain that value?
How many displayed intervals cover the reference value {reference_value}?
entirely_above_reference_count: How many category intervals are entirely above reference value {reference_value}?
Count the intervals whose lower endpoint is greater than {reference_value}.
Using the printed endpoints, how many categories have intervals fully above {reference_value}?
For reference value {reference_value}, how many interval whiskers lie completely above it?
How many displayed intervals exclude {reference_value} on the high side?
entirely_below_reference_count: How many category intervals are entirely below reference value {reference_value}?
Count the intervals whose upper endpoint is less than {reference_value}.
Using the printed endpoints, how many categories have intervals fully below {reference_value}?
For reference value {reference_value}, how many interval whiskers lie completely below it?
How many displayed intervals exclude {reference_value} on the low side?
widest_interval_label: Using upper minus lower endpoint, what category label has the {relation_phrase}?
Compare interval widths. Which category has the {relation_phrase}?
Find the category with the {relation_phrase}. What is its label?
What category label corresponds to the {relation_phrase}?
narrowest_interval_label: Using upper minus lower endpoint, what category label has the {relation_phrase}?
Compare interval widths. Which category has the {relation_phrase}?
Find the category with the {relation_phrase}. What is its label?
What category label corresponds to the {relation_phrase}?
second_widest_interval_label: Using upper minus lower endpoint, what category label has the {relation_phrase}?
Compare interval widths. Which category has the {relation_phrase}?
Find the category with the {relation_phrase}. What is its label?
What category label corresponds to the {relation_phrase}?
second_narrowest_interval_label: Using upper minus lower endpoint, what category label has the {relation_phrase}?
Compare interval widths. Which category has the {relation_phrase}?
Find the category with the {relation_phrase}. What is its label?
What category label corresponds to the {relation_phrase}?

Answer format: {answer_hint}.

Required answer format: {answer_hint}.

query:contains_reference_count.answer_hint: set "answer" to the requested integer count
query:contains_reference_count.annotation_hint: set "annotation" to an array of lower-to-upper interval segments, each segment written as [[x0,y0],[x1,y1]] for every interval that satisfies the condition

query:entirely_above_reference_count.answer_hint: set "answer" to the requested integer count
query:entirely_above_reference_count.annotation_hint: set "annotation" to an array of lower-to-upper interval segments, each segment written as [[x0,y0],[x1,y1]] for every interval that satisfies the condition

query:entirely_below_reference_count.answer_hint: set "answer" to the requested integer count
query:entirely_below_reference_count.annotation_hint: set "annotation" to an array of lower-to-upper interval segments, each segment written as [[x0,y0],[x1,y1]] for every interval that satisfies the condition

query:widest_interval_label.answer_hint: set "answer" to the requested category label as a string
query:widest_interval_label.annotation_hint: set "annotation" to the selected interval's lower-to-upper segment as [[x0,y0],[x1,y1]]

query:narrowest_interval_label.answer_hint: set "answer" to the requested category label as a string
query:narrowest_interval_label.annotation_hint: set "annotation" to the selected interval's lower-to-upper segment as [[x0,y0],[x1,y1]]

query:second_widest_interval_label.answer_hint: set "answer" to the requested category label as a string
query:second_widest_interval_label.annotation_hint: set "annotation" to the selected interval's lower-to-upper segment as [[x0,y0],[x1,y1]]

query:second_narrowest_interval_label.answer_hint: set "answer" to the requested category label as a string
query:second_narrowest_interval_label.annotation_hint: set "annotation" to the selected interval's lower-to-upper segment as [[x0,y0],[x1,y1]]
